mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, boosting consumer confidence and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong manufacturing output combined with robust housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which increases corporate revenue visibility and supports equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO > 105 and HOUST > 1500 fails to produce positive forward returns in at least 60% of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where inflation expectations accelerate faster than policy rates, real yields rise and compress equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS growth by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and financial assets indicate expanding household purchasing power and wealth effects, which directly translate into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS against 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below the 100 baseline signals supply-side bottlenecks or demand destruction, which reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 100.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market tightening, which accelerates wage-driven consumption and pushes equity markets higher over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered windows over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth outpaces headline inflation, real corporate cash flows expand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where quarterly GDPC1 growth exceeds CPIAUCSL inflation by more than 1.5 percentage points.
